message.text: IAM role deploy_admin: permissions_boundary unset. Future policy attachments can grant unlimited privilege — the role's effective permissions = union of all attached policies, no ceiling.
IAM policy ci_runner_perms: grants a high-privilege action with no Condition block. Compromise of any principal holding this policy gives unconstrained use of that action.

IAM policy deploy_admin_perms: enables privilege escalation — full administrator wildcard (Action: "*") — granted on an unconstrained resource (Resource: "*") with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can escalate to broader or administrator privileges.

Role-chaining path on role ci_runner: it is assumable by a compute service (EC2 / ECS / Lambda), and can sts:AssumeRole its way to role deploy_admin, which grants full administrator wildcard (Action: "*"). An attacker who reaches ci_runner can pivot through role assumption to those permissions — even though neither ci_runner's own permissions nor deploy_admin's trust policy looks dangerous in isolation.

IAM policy deploy_admin_perms: grants data exfiltration actions (s3:GetObject, dynamodb:Scan, dynamodb:GetItem, dynamodb:BatchGetItem, dynamodb:Query, kinesis:GetRecords, sqs:ReceiveMessage, rds:DownloadDBLogFilePortion, athena:GetQueryResults, glue:GetSecurityConfiguration) on Resource: "*" with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can bulk-read or export data across all accessible resources of that service — without needing to escalate privileges first.

IAM policy deploy_admin_perms: grants credential extraction actions (secretsmanager:GetSecretValue, secretsmanager:BatchGetSecretValue, ssm:GetParameter, ssm:GetParameters, ssm:GetParametersByPath, kms:Decrypt, kms:GenerateDataKey, sts:GetSessionToken, sts:GetFederationToken) on Resource: "*" with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can read secrets, SSM parameters, or decrypt KMS-protected data across all resources in the account.

IAM policy deploy_admin_perms: grants resource-exposure actions (s3:PutBucketPolicy, s3:PutBucketAcl, s3:PutObjectAcl, lambda:AddPermission, lambda:AddLayerVersionPermission, sqs:AddPermission, sns:AddPermission, secretsmanager:PutResourcePolicy, kms:PutKeyPolicy, ecr:SetRepositoryPolicy) on Resource: "*" with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can modify the access policy of S3 buckets, Lambda functions, SQS queues, KMS keys, or other resources — potentially making them publicly accessible.

IAM policy deploy_admin_perms: grants infrastructure-modification actions (ec2:RunInstances, ec2:RequestSpotInstances, ecs:RunTask, eks:CreateCluster, rds:CreateDBInstance, rds:RestoreDBInstanceFromDBSnapshot, ec2:AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress, ec2:CreateVpc, ec2:CreateKeyPair) on Resource: "*" with no Condition. Any principal holding this policy can launch EC2 instances, run ECS tasks, or modify network rules without restriction — a common cryptomining and lateral-movement vector.
IAM role ci_runner: permissions_boundary unset. Future policy attachments can grant unlimited privilege — the role's effective permissions = union of all attached policies, no ceiling.
